title: '--cap-drop ALL' broken in podman-2.1.x
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I have a root-executed container run with argument '`--cap-drop ALL`' which has worked as anticipated with all `podman-2.0.x` releases.  However, with `podman-2.1.0` (and the latest `seccomp.json` from containers/common) on launch I get:

```
Error: container_linux.go:370: starting container process caused: apply caps: operation not permitted: OCI runtime permission denied error
```

... which goes away if I _downgrade_ back to `podman-2.0.6`.

The invocation (as root) is:

```
podman run --cap-drop ALL --mount type=bind,source=/var/run/irqbalance,destination=/var/run/irqbalance --name openrc-irqbalance-1.6.0-r1 --network none --tty service.sys-apps.irqbalance:1.6.0-r1 -d -f -s /var/run/irqbalance/irqbalance.pid
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

Git Commit:   d8868746fd3f45b9bfd79e37bf2aa0c7103245fd

OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
Hm. Can't reproduce over here.

@rhatdan @baude @vrothberg Can anyone else try?
If you disable seccomp does it work?

I have a Gentoo system also, 5.4.60-gentoo, libpod-2.1.0 as of this morning, and cannot reproduce this:
```console
# podman run --cap-drop ALL --network none --name openrc-irqbalance-1.6.0-r1  --rm  --tty --mount type=bind,source=/var/tmp,destination=/var/tmp alpine grep Cap /proc/self/status
CapInh: 0000000000000000
CapPrm: 0000000000000000
CapEff: 0000000000000000
CapBnd: 0000000000000000
CapAmb: 0000000000000000
```

> If you disable seccomp does it work?

By adding `--security-opt=seccomp=unconfined`?

> I have a Gentoo system also, 5.4.60-gentoo, libpod-2.1.0 as of this morning, and cannot reproduce this:
> 
> ```
> # podman run --cap-drop ALL --network none --name openrc-irqbalance-1.6.0-r1  --rm  --tty --mount type=bind,source=/var/tmp,destination=/var/tmp alpine grep Cap /proc/self/status
> CapInh: 0000000000000000
> CapPrm: 0000000000000000
> CapEff: 0000000000000000
> CapBnd: 0000000000000000
> CapAmb: 0000000000000000
> ```

Interesting - I'm running kernel 5.8.9 and running:

```
sudo podman run --cap-drop ALL --mount type=bind,source=/var/run/irqbalance,destination=/var/run/irqbalance --name openrc-irqbalance-1.6.0-r1 --network none --tty --entrypoint=/bin/sh alpine
```

... works as expected.  But running:

```
sudo podman run --cap-drop ALL --mount type=bind,source=/var/run/irqbalance,destination=/var/run/irqbalance --name openrc-irqbalance-1.6.0-r1 --network none --tty --entrypoint=/bin/sh service.sys-apps.irqbalance:1.6.0-r1
```

(e.g. the same thing with a different image) causes the above error and leaves the container in `Created` state (meaning that the container was never entered?)

*But* the same image works correctly with `podman-2.0.6`.

The above `security-opt` argument seems to make no difference, so this might not be the right way to disable seccomp?

As a side-note, even with `podman-2.1.0`, leaving out the '`--cap-drop ALL`' also allows the container to run as expected:

```
$ sudo podman run -i --mount type=bind,source=/var/run/irqbalance,destination=/var/run/irqbalance --name openrc-irqbalance-1.6.0-r1 --network none --tty --entrypoint=/bin/sh service.sys-apps.irqbalance:1.6.0-r1
/ # 
```

Where is this error actually being generated from?

Within the `podman` project, `container_linux.go` isn't long enough and `container_internal_linux.go` (which may not be relevant?) seems to be doing container spec generation (specifically processing the `$HOME` environment-variable) at that point in 2.1.0?

That message is definitely coming out of `runc` - I've seen that error format before (and the `OCI runtime permission denied error` suffix from Podman means the error was produced by the OCI runtime).

The error points to https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/blob/master/libcontainer/container_linux.go#L370 which looks about right - a failure to start the user process, due to a failure to set capabilities, would happen there.
